title: Stored/Reflected XSS via Unsanitized header_text/footer_text in HTML Export Preview
vulnerability_description: The HTML export formatter writes user-controlled settings values (header_text, footer_text) into the generated HTML document without any sanitization. When a user triggers the 'Preview' action in the plugin admin UI, the server generates an HTML document containing these values and returns it in the JSON response field 'html'. The front-end JavaScript injects this directly into the admin page DOM using jQuery.html(), causing any embedded JavaScript to execute in the admin's browser context.

affected_components.description: header_text and footer_text inserted into HTML output without escaping
ajax_preview entry point returning $html from ob_get_contents()

taint_flow.step4: ob_get_contents() captures the HTML including the unsanitized header_text into $html
taint_flow.step5: echo json_encode(array('total' => $total, 'html' => $html)) sends it as JSON

auth_context.capability_notes: view_woocommerce_reports is granted to WooCommerce Shop Manager role — not administrator-only
auth_context.nonce_obtainable: Yes — embedded in JavaScript on any WooCommerce admin page the user can access (settings_form.woe_nonce)

attack_scenarios.description: A Shop Manager crafts a preview AJAX request with malicious header_text. The XSS executes in their own browser. Limited impact unless used in a CSRF chain.

A Shop Manager saves an export profile (via ajax_save_settings) with malicious header_text in the settings. When any Administrator views the preview of that profile, the stored XSS fires in the Administrator's browser, potentially stealing session cookies or performing privileged actions.

poc.notes: The format must be HTML (not CSV/XLS/etc.) because the JS at buttons.js:13-21 uses jQuery.text() for flat formats and jQuery.html() only for non-flat formats. HTML is the only format that reaches the .html() sink.

remediation.recommendation: Apply wp_kses() or esc_html() to header_text and footer_text before writing them to the HTML output in WOE_Formatter_Html. Alternatively, escape them in make_new_settings() before storing in $settings.
remediation.note_on_custom_css: custom_css is also inserted into a <style> block without sanitization. This allows CSS injection. While CSS injection is less severe than JS XSS, consider sanitizing or restricting this field to admins only.
